summary: The charge stored on a capacitor is proportional to the voltage across it: Q = C·U. The capacitance C is the constant of proportionality.
method: Identify the capacitor and the two known quantities.
Decide which quantity is unknown — charge, capacitance, or voltage.
Rearrange Q = C·U for the unknown.
Use SI units (coulombs, farads, volts); real capacitances are often microfarads, so convert µF to F.

example.title: Charge on a capacitor
steps: The unknown is Q, so use Q = C·U.
